stories.title: Apple’s failed self-driving car program left a legacy of powerful AI chips
stories.simple_summary: Apple's attempt to build a self-driving car didn't succeed, but the powerful computer chips they developed for it turned out to be great for AI tasks. Those chips are now used in Apple's everyday products.
stories.why_it_matters: This doesn't affect you directly — it's mostly about why Apple's gadgets are good at AI tasks, which you probably already notice.

stories.title: The fight against AI data centers is just beginning
stories.simple_summary: Communities and towns are starting to push back against building large AI data centers (huge warehouses full of computers) because they use enormous amounts of electricity.
stories.why_it_matters: If you live near where a data center might be built, this could affect your local power costs and energy availability.

stories.title: OpenAI bets on families as ChatGPT goes deeper into households
stories.simple_summary: OpenAI (the company behind ChatGPT) is hiring someone to make ChatGPT work better for families, caregivers, and older adults.
stories.why_it_matters: This means ChatGPT might become easier for you and your family to use in the future.

stories.title: Meta removes controversial AI feature on Instagram after backlash
stories.simple_summary: Instagram removed a new AI feature after people complained about it. The feature let users create fake AI images based on real Instagram posts from public accounts.
stories.why_it_matters: Your Instagram photos are safer now — Instagram won't turn them into AI pictures without your permission.

stories.title: Meta turns off the Instagram feature that let users make AI deepfakes of public accounts
stories.simple_summary: Meta turned off an Instagram feature that let people use anyone's public photos to make fake AI images of them without asking permission first.
stories.why_it_matters: Your social media photos are better protected — people can't easily create fake AI pictures of you anymore.

stories.title: Apple sues OpenAI for allegedly stealing hardware secrets
stories.simple_summary: Apple is suing OpenAI, claiming that former Apple employees stole Apple's secret information about hardware (physical equipment) and gave it to OpenAI.
stories.why_it_matters: This is mainly a legal battle between two big tech companies — it doesn't directly affect you, but it shows companies are protective of their secrets.

stories.title: Apple sues OpenAI over alleged trade secret theft
stories.simple_summary: Apple filed a lawsuit against OpenAI, alleging that current and former Apple workers gave OpenAI secret information about how to build hardware.
stories.why_it_matters: This is a business dispute between major companies — nothing you need to worry about.

stories.title: Open source AI matters more than ever, according to Hugging Face’s Clem Delangue
stories.simple_summary: Open source AI (free AI software that anyone can use and modify) is growing fast. About half of big Fortune 500 companies now use it.
stories.why_it_matters: This means AI tools are becoming more available to smaller businesses and individuals, not just giant corporations.

stories.title: SK Hynix raises $26.5B in the biggest foreign IPO in US history, is urged to build new US fabs
stories.simple_summary: A computer chip company called SK Hynix raised $26.5 billion in the stock market (its biggest financial event), and now people are asking them to build factories in the United States.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to investors and the economy — building new factories could create local jobs in some areas.

stories.title: Hugging Face’s CEO on why companies are done renting their AI
stories.simple_summary: Companies are starting to prefer using free, open source AI tools instead of renting AI from big companies like OpenAI or Google.
stories.why_it_matters: Small businesses may have cheaper AI options available to them in the future.

stories.title: Instagram’s Adam Mosseri: If you don’t like AI, ‘then you shouldn’t have it in your feed’
stories.simple_summary: Instagram's leader says the platform won't hide AI-generated content, but you'll be able to tell which posts are AI-made so you can choose not to see them.
stories.why_it_matters: You'll have a choice about whether you want to see AI content in your Instagram feed.

stories.title: Would you host part of an AI data center in your home?
stories.simple_summary: A solar energy company is testing a program where they pay homeowners to put small AI computer equipment in their homes to help run AI services.
stories.why_it_matters: You could potentially earn money by letting a company put computer equipment in your house, though this is just a test program right now.
